questions.q: Which operator is used to insert data into the cout stream?
questions.explanation: The insertion operator << "pushes" data into the output stream.

questions.q: Which data type is best suited for storing a single character like 'A' or '$'?
questions.explanation: char is specifically designed for single characters, whereas string is used for sequences of text.

questions.q: What is the correct way to end a statement in C++?
questions.explanation: Much like a period ends a sentence in English, the semicolon ; tells the compiler that a statement is finished.

questions.q: Which of these is the correct header file to include for input and output?
questions.explanation: iostream stands for "input-output stream" and is necessary for using cin and cout.

questions.q: What is the index of the very first element in a C++ array?
questions.explanation: C++ uses zero-based indexing, meaning we start counting from 0.
In C++, MyVariable and myvariable are treated as two completely different identifiers.

questions.q: The int data type is used to store decimal numbers like 3.14.
questions.explanation: int is for integers (whole numbers). To store decimals, you should use float or double.

questions.q: Comments in C++ are ignored by the compiler.
questions.explanation: Comments (starting with // or /* */) are only there for humans to read.

questions.q: Every C++ program must have a function named main().
questions.explanation: The main() function is the entry point of the program where execution begins.

questions.q: You can change the value of a variable declared with the const keyword.
questions.explanation: const stands for "constant," meaning the value is read-only once it is set.

questions.q: Declare an integer variable named age and assign it the value 25.
questions.explanation: You must specify the type (int), the name (age), and use the assignment operator (=).

questions.q: Write the syntax for a single-line comment.
questions.explanation: Anything following // on that same line is ignored by the compiler.

questions.q: Write a code snippet that takes an integer input from the user and stores it in a variable called userNum.
questions.explanation: cin uses the extraction operator >> to pull data from the keyboard into a variable.

questions.q: Write a basic if statement that checks if a variable x is greater than 10.
questions.explanation: The condition must be enclosed in parentheses, and the code to execute is usually placed inside curly braces.

questions.q: Write the line of code that allows you to use cout and cin without typing std:: every time.
questions.explanation: This tells the compiler to look in the standard (std) namespace for identifiers.
